title: Molecular Cartography/Resolve OCT Sectioning
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e59p2","text":"Protocol for sectioning OCT samples for Molecular Cartography/Resolve.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set cryostat to -20° C. Place samples inside to acclimate to temperatures for 10 minutes.
Add OCT media to the chuck and place the sample block on top. Let freeze for 5 minutes.
Insert and fasten cryostat blade and set sectioning thickness to 30-60 um.
Place chuck in specimen station and adjust sample distance and angle to blade.
Trim sample block until desired depth is reached.
Lightly score block with a razor blade so that tissue fits within the designated scan areas on the 8-sample Resolve slides.
Section at 10 um (or other desired thickness) and place section within the a scan area of the slide.
Repeat as needed to fill the slide.
Store slides at -80°C directly after sectioning.
creator: Joanna Bi
